{{Quote|Getting old, perhaps I am...After so many years of relative peace, troubles me does a threat like this. Fly into danger, they do...But face uncertainty also do we who remain|[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]|<ref name="Jedi Council: Acts of War 1">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 1|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 1]]</ref>}}
The '''second mission to the Yinchorri system''' in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] mission sent to the [[Yinchorri system]] following the [[First mission to the Yinchorri system|deaths]] of the Jedi [[Naeshahn]] and her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Ebor Taulk]], who had been sent to investigate [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] aggression against the neighboring systems.

==Background==
[[File:WinduJediBriefing-ActsOfWar1.jpg|thumb|left|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] briefing the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].]]

The Jedi mission consisted of twelve Jedi and was made up of three groups, each assigned with a [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class space cruiser]] since there were only three habitable worlds in the system.

[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] were assigned with visiting the harsh Yinchorri homeworld of [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] while [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]] and [[Theen Fida]] were assigned to the ocean world of [[Yitheeth]]. [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], [[Micah Giiett]], [[Lilit Twoseas]] and [[K'Kruhk]] were sent to [[Yibikkoror]], a tiny world with a dense atmosphere. All three groups would search for a rumored Yinchorri command center.

==The mission==
Having jumped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and arrived at the Yinchorri system, they were immediately attacked by [[Yinchorri starfighter]]s. The Jedi were disadvantaged since their cruisers were unarmed.

On Adi Gallia's cruiser, the Tholothain Jedi would admit that she wished the cruisers were armed. Tsui Choi would reply by saying that it was spoken by the daughter of a diplomat. Their cruiser than succeeded in destroying two of the fighter by tricking a third into destroying them before proceeding to Yitheeth.

[[File:Mission_to_the_Yinchorri_system2.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A [[Yinchorri starfighter]] firing on an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]].]]

Meanwhile on Mace Windu's cruiser, the enemy fighter disabled its shields though its occupants evaded capture thanks to Saesee Tiin, who made a jump into hyperspace past a planet. To their Yinchorri pursuers, it appeared that their target had made a jump into hyperspace through the planet. By the time they found out, it was too late since the cruiser had headed to Yinchorr.

At Micah Giiett's cruiser, having failed to shake off their Yinchorri pursuers, the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] K'Kruhk proposed to the other Jedi that they trick the Yinchorri into thinking that they had left the cruiser by releasing all the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] and the salon pod. The plan worked perfectly and the Yinchorri fighters went after them, allowing the cruiser to evade them and land at a floating platform on gassy Yibikkoror. The Jedi barely escaped their Yinchorri pursuers, though they all managed to reach their destinations.

==Aftermath==
On Yitheeth and Yibikkoror, after brief confrontations with Yinchorri warriors, the two groups realized that the command center was not on their planet. Tsui Choi's group traveled to Yibikkoror, and picked up the other group, which was having trouble fighting the Yinchorri.

*[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 1|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 1]]
